memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Frost, Froste and others. First found in Hampshire where William Frost was recorded in the Domesday Book in 1086 C.E. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Nicholas Frost, who came to Maine in 1630; William Frost, who arrived in New York State in 1630; Thomas Frost settled in St. Christopher in 1635; Edmund Frost, a church Elder, on record in Cambridge, MA in 1635. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). This ancient name found in the spellings of Forst and Frost, is of Anglo-Saxon origin, and has the unusual distinction of being recorded as a surname as early as 1086 (in the Domesday Book), and also of having retained its original forms through the millennium since. The name derives from the Old English pre-7th Century "frost or forst", and it is claimed was used as a nickname for one who had white hair or a white beard, or who was of 'frosty' disposition. These are the 'accepted' academic theories among those in the ivory towers of education, however logic suggests the opposite. We believe from our experience that 'Frost or Forst' had a quite different meaning, and was a baptismal name applied to one born in the times of frost, generally autumn. It is obvious that the name was considered complimentary, or it would not have survived, and this is confirmed by its paramount position among the earliest of all surnames. The very early recordings include Lefstan Frost in the Feudal Documents of the Abbey of Bury St. Edmunds, Suffolk, in 1095, and Gilbert Frost of Warwick in the 1195 pipe rolls. Later examples include John Frost of Cambridge (1626 - 1667), a puritan divine and associate of Oliver Cromwell, and Thomas Frost aged 28 yrs, who was one of the very earliest settlers in the New World Colonies, leaving London on the ship "John" in October 1635 bound for St Christopher in the West Indies. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of William Forst, which was dated 1086, in the Domesday Book of Hampshire, during the reign of King William 1st, known as "The Conqueror", 1066 - 1087. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Frost於1594年12月1日出生在英國素福爾可斯坦斯亞德時，她的父親Edward Frost11591歲，母親Thomasine Belgrave34歲。她於1612年11月19日與Thomas Blower在英國素福爾可斯坦斯亞德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1649年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波享年55歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Frost于1594年12月1日出生在英国素福爾可斯坦斯亞德时，她的父亲Edward Frost11591岁，母亲Thomasine Belgrave34岁。她于1612年11月19日与Thomas Blower在英国素福爾可斯坦斯亞德结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1649年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波享年55岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼巴恩斯塔波。

nameMeanings.name: Frost
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Danish, and Swedish: nickname for someone who suffers from being cold, or perhaps used in the sense ‘frosty, cold as frost, without warmth of feeling’, or perhaps ‘having the appearance of being covered with frost’ for one with white hair or a white beard. From Old English, Old High German, Old Norse frost ‘frost’.

variants.name: Chell
meanings: English (Staffordshire): from an unrecorded Middle English male or female personal name Chell(e) or Chele, Old English Cēolla, Cēol, or Cēola, short forms of names beginning with Cēol- ‘ship’, such as Cēolbeorht (male) and Cēolflǣd (female). Alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English chele, chile (Old English cīele, cēle, cȳle) ‘cold, lacking sympathy’. Compare Frost .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Frost was born on 1 December 1594, in Stanstead, Suffolk, England, her father, Edward Frost, was 11591 and her mother, Thomasine Belgrave, was 34. She married Thomas Blower on 19 November 1612, in Stanstead, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 6 daughters. She died in 1649, in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 55, and was buried in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Frost est née le 1 décembre 1594, à Stanstead, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Edward Frost, avait 11591 ans et sa mère, Thomasine Belgrave, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Blower le 19 novembre 1612, à Stanstead, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée en 1649, à Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Frost am 1. Dezember 1594 in Stanstead, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edward Frost 11591 und ihre Mutter Thomasine Belgrave 34. Sie hat am 19. November 1612 in Stanstead, Suffolk, England Thomas Blower geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb 1649 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Frost è nata il il 1 dicembre 1594 nel Stanstead, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Edward Frost, aveva 11591 anni e sua madre, Thomasine Belgrave, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Blower il 19 novembre 1612, nel Stanstead, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel 1649, nel Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolta a Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Frostが1594年12月1日にイギリススフォルクスタンステアドで生まれた時，父Edward Frostは11591歳，母Thomasine Belgraveは34歳であった。1612年11月19日にイギリススフォルクスタンステアドでThomas Blowerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1649年にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーバーンスタブルで55歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーバーンスタブルにて埋葬された。

ko.Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Ba: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 스프링필드
ko.Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일
ko.All Saints, Sudbury, England: 영국 수폴크 수드버이 앨 세인츠' 처르치
ko.St. Gregory, Sudbury, Suffolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 수드버이 스트 그레고리
ko.lifeSummary: Alice Frost(이)가 1594년 12월 1일 영국 수폴크 스탠스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward Frost(은)는 11591세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Thomasine Belgrave(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1612년 11월 19일 영국 수폴크 스탠스테아드에서 Thomas Blower(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1649년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 바른스타블일에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Frost nasceu em 1 de dezembro de 1594, em Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward Frost, tinha 11591 anos e sua mãe, Thomasine Belgrave, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Blower em 19 de novembro de 1612, em Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1649, em Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Frost родилась 1 декабря 1594 г. в местности станстид, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Edward Frost было 11591 год, а матери, которую звали Thomasine Belgrave, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Blower 19 ноября 1612 г. в местности станстид, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла в 1649 г. в местности барнстабл, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности барнстабл, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

Se casó con Thomas Blower el 19 de noviembre de 1612, en Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió en 1649, en Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Barnstable, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.
